Meaning.En: The amount of matter in an object, typically measured in kilograms.
Meaning.Example: The mass of the planet Earth is approximately 5.97 × 10²⁴ kilograms.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả lượng vật chất trong vật thể trong vật lý.
Situation.En: Describing the quantity of matter in a physical object in physics.

Meaning.En: A large body of matter with no definite shape; a large number of people or things gathered together.
Meaning.Example: A mass of people gathered in the square to protest.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả một nhóm người đông đảo hoặc vật chất không định hình.
Situation.En: Describing a large crowd of people or unshaped matter.

Meaning.En: The central religious service in the Roman Catholic Church, involving the Eucharist.
Meaning.Example: She attends Mass every Sunday at 9 a.m.

Situation.Vi: Thực hành phụng vụ Thánh lễ trong Giáo hội Công giáo.
Situation.En: Participating in the Catholic Eucharistic liturgy.

Meaning.En: To determine the mass of something, especially using scientific instruments.
Meaning.Example: The scientist will mass the sample before the experiment.

Situation.Vi: Thao tác đo khối lượng trong phòng thí nghiệm.
Situation.En: Measuring the mass of a substance in a laboratory setting.

Meaning.En: To gather or collect (people or things) into a dense group or mass.
Meaning.Example: They massed their troops near the border.

Situation.Vi: Di chuyển và tập trung quân đội hoặc lực lượng.
Situation.En: Moving and concentrating troops or resources in one area.
